title: Rules of Hooks and `useState` Recap

learningObjectives.text: Explain rules of hooks using a new example and justify the result.
Apply state initialization and updates using a new example and justify the result.
Choose and justify when to use state using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns rules of hooks and `usestate` recap from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Effects and Hooks** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Rules of Hooks** → **State initialization and updates** → **When to use state**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Rules of Hooks
sections.body: Call Hooks at the top level of React components or custom Hooks, never conditionally or inside ordinary functions.

sections.title: State initialization and updates
sections.body: Use lazy initialization for expensive initial state and updater functions for transitions based on previous state.

sections.title: When to use state
sections.body: Use state for changing information that affects rendering, ordinary variables for render calculations, and refs for mutable non-rendered values.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: Lazy initialization runs once and the updater creates the next object.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Rules of Hooks and `useState` Recap connects Rules of Hooks, State initialization and updates, When to use state.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
